item.title: Free OnlyFans Lure Used to Spread Cross-Platform CRPx0 Malware
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend
item.description:
Campaign uses fake OnlyFans account downloads to infect Windows and macOS systems with multi-function malware.

The post Free OnlyFans Lure Used to Spread Cross-Platform CRPx0 Malware appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Deal Reached With Hackers to Delete Data Stolen From the Canvas Educational Platform
item.dc:creator: Associated Press
item.description:
The company that operates online learning system Canvas said it struck a deal with hackers to delete the data they pilfered in a cyberattack that created chaos for students, many of them in the middle of finals.

The post Deal Reached With Hackers to Delete Data Stolen From the Canvas Educational Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: West Pharmaceutical Services Hit by Disruptive Ransomware Attack
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
The company took systems offline globally after hackers exfiltrated data and deployed file-encrypting ransomware.

The post West Pharmaceutical Services Hit by Disruptive Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities in macOS, iOS
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
The tech giant has also ported the patch for a recent deleted chats recovery issue to older versions of iOS.

The post Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities in macOS, iOS appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: SAP Patches Critical S/4HANA, Commerce Vulnerabilities
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
The flaws could allow attackers to inject malicious code, leading to information disclosure and code execution.

The post SAP Patches Critical S/4HANA, Commerce Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What It Really Means
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
Curl’s lead developer says Mythos claims are marketing, but many in the industry believe the results stem from Curl’s robust security.

The post Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What It Really Means appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Is the SOC Obsolete, and We Just Haven’t Admitted It Yet?
item.dc:creator: Danelle Au
item.description:
Many AI-first enterprises have already embraced sovereign architectures for general AI initiatives; cybersecurity—and the SOC—should be next.

The post Is the SOC Obsolete, and We Just Haven’t Admitted It Yet? appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: TanStack, Mistral AI, UiPath Hit in Fresh Supply Chain Attack
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
Over 400 malicious versions of 170 packages were published as part of the new Mini Shai-Hulud campaign.

The post TanStack, Mistral AI, UiPath Hit in Fresh Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Frame Security Emerges From Stealth With $50M for Awareness and Training Platform
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
Team8, Index Ventures, Picture Capital, Elad Gil, Cerca Partners, and Tesonet invested in Frame Security.

The post Frame Security Emerges From Stealth With $50M for Awareness and Training Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend
item.description:
Rather than scanning code alone, Build Application Firewalls inspect runtime behavior inside the software build pipeline.

The post Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
